message:
feat(frankenterm-web): add event subscriptions, OSC 9;4 progress signals, extract pane gestures

Implement deterministic event subscription infrastructure and progress
signal mapping for FrankenTermWeb, plus extract shared pane interaction
logic for terminal/WASM behavioral parity.

Event subscriptions (bd-1q5.13):
- Add createEventSubscription/eventSubscriptionState/drainEventSubscription
  and drainEventSubscriptionJsonl API methods
- Implement HostEventType enum covering 15 host-observable event classes
  including new TerminalProgress event type
- Add globally monotonic seq field for deterministic per-subscription FIFO
  ordering across subscribed event types
- Bounded buffering: configurable queues (512-8192 items), max 256
  subscriptions to prevent unbounded memory growth
- Accessibility announcement queue (max 64) with dedicated drain paths
- Add tracing dependency for event subscription lifecycle diagnostics

Progress signal mapping (bd-2vr05.14.4):
- Implement OSC 9;4 terminal.progress event emission with
  ProgressSignalState enum (Remove/Normal/Error/Indeterminate/Warning)
- Deterministic normalization with explicit state encoding rules
- Parse and validate progress sequences; emit rejection envelopes for
  malformed input with bounded delivery policy
- Track progress_last_value for stateful value retention across resets

Pane interaction refactoring (bd-1k7c5):
- Extract shared gesture types (PaneGestureMode, ActivePaneGesture,
  PaneMovePolicyInput) and functions into ftui-demo-showcase::pane_interaction
- Remove 107 lines of duplicate gesture logic from ftui-showcase-wasm
  runner_core.rs; WASM adapter now shares terminal-first canonical code path
- Add pane-parity-contract-and-program.md specification

Documentation and validation:
- Update frankenterm-web-api.md: document event subscriptions, progress
  signals, ordering contract (8 -> 10 guarantees), bounded buffering
- Add frankenterm_event_ordering_check.mjs E2E script validating FIFO
  guarantees, required event types, and subscription API completeness
- Add golden_frame_e2e.rs test framework for deterministic replay

Beads: close bd-2vr05.14.2 (OSC-8 hyperlinks), bd-2vr05.14.3 (inline
images), bd-2vr05.14.4 (progress signals); add bd-2oe6g (wasm-pack
getrandom build failure).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2o55.4","title":"Accessibility Modes — E2E PTY Tests (Verbose Logs)","description":"PTY E2E tests with detailed logs for Accessibility Modes, including timing/interaction traces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Accessibility Modes — E2E PTY Tests (Verbose Logs)
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)

+{"id":"bd-2oe6g","title":"bug(frankenterm-web): wasm-pack E2E build fails due getrandom wasm32 config","description":"Background\n- Running tests/e2e/scripts/test_frankenterm_event_ordering_contract.sh currently fails before fixture execution.\n- wasm-pack build for frankenterm-web errors in getrandom 0.3.4 for wasm32-unknown-unknown requiring wasm_js config.\n\nGoal\n- Restore deterministic event-ordering E2E execution by fixing wasm target config/dependency path.\n\nAcceptance Criteria\n- wasm-pack build --target nodejs succeeds for crates/frankenterm-web in CI/E2E environment.\n- tests/e2e/scripts/test_frankenterm_event_ordering_contract.sh runs to completion and produces summary/jsonl artifacts.\n- Fix is documented in relevant Cargo/config docs and does not regress native checks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-16T15:59:47.699875995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T15:59:47.699875995Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","frankenterm","getrandom","toolchain","wasm"]}
 {"id":"bd-2oovu","title":"Complexity Guards + Degradation Strategy","description":"Background
- Large or pathological graphs can blow up layout/routing cost.
- We need deterministic guardrails and graceful degradation.

Scope
- Define complexity limits: max nodes/edges, route search budget, layout iterations.
- Compute a diagram complexity score (nodes, edges, labels, clusters).
- Degradation modes: hide labels, collapse clusters, simplify routing, reduce decoration, or ASCII fallback.
- Log guard triggers + chosen degradation in JSONL with codes.

Considerations
- Guardrails must be deterministic and based on config.
- Degradation should preserve readability and avoid crashes.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for guard triggers at thresholds.
- Snapshot tests showing degraded outputs.
- PTY E2E logs verify guard activation.

Success Criteria
- Engine never crashes on oversized inputs; produces predictable degraded output.","notes":"Plan draft: add MermaidComplexity report (nodes, edges, clusters, labels, ports, link_count, label_chars/lines) + score; compare to MermaidConfig limits (max_nodes/max_edges/max_label_*, route_budget, layout_iteration_budget) and emit warnings + degradation plan (hide labels, collapse clusters, simplify routing, ASCII fallback). Likely store in MermaidDiagramMeta or new MermaidDegradationPlan struct for downstream renderer + JSONL logging. No code edits yet; waiting on IR readiness to avoid conflicts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayDune","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:58:36.430234002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:57.037940407Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:57.037920439Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oovu","depends_on_id":"bd-1ri6i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:00.557780971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2oovu","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:00.785153355Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2oovu.1","title":"Docs: Mermaid complexity guards + degradation strategy","description":"Document complexity scoring, thresholds, and deterministic degradation modes for Mermaid diagrams. Place in docs/spec/mermaid-config.md.","notes":"Blocked: docs/spec/mermaid-config.md reserved by GrayDune; will resume after reservation clears.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"SunnyWolf","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:23:10.656435771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:08:29.705144825Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:08:29.705126010Z","close_reason":"Documented complexity formulas, thresholds, and deterministic degradation modes in docs/spec/mermaid-config.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oovu.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2oovu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:23:10.656435771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2orqr","title":"Diagram Type: State Diagram (v2)","description":"Background
- State diagrams are core to architecture and control flow docs.

Scope
- Parse stateDiagram-v2 syntax (states, transitions, composite states, start/end nodes).
- Support notes and internal transitions.
- Layout with nested boxes for composite states.
- Route transitions with orthogonal edges and labels.

Considerations
- Nested layout must preserve boundaries and padding.
- Transitions crossing state boundaries should be routed around edges.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for nested state parsing/layout.
- Snapshot tests at multiple sizes.
- PTY E2E logs: diagram_type=state, size, tier, warnings.

Success Criteria
- Nested states render without overlap.
- Transitions readable at small sizes.","notes":"Initial audit: stateDiagram-v2 currently parsed via generic graph path in mermaid.rs; no [*], state blocks, or notes parsing yet. Plan: extend parser for start/end nodes ([*]), state declarations (state X / state "Label" as X), composite blocks (state X { ... }), and note lines; map to IR with cluster membership + implicit start/end nodes; update layout to keep nested boundaries; add fixtures/snapshots for nested states + start/end; ensure deterministic ordering + label wrap. Waiting on mermaid.rs/mermaid_layout.rs reservation window (TurquoiseBeacon).

Implementation sketch:
- Add Statement::StateDecl / StateBlockStart / StateBlockEnd / StateNote (single + multiline note...end note).
- Parse in stateDiagram branch before parse_edge/parse_node.
- Start/end: detect `[*]` tokens; map to synthetic ids using span (e.g., __start_L{line}_C{col}) with label "*".
- Composite: create cluster draft with title (label or id). Add hidden node class (e.g., "composite_container") for edge targets; renderer skips drawing nodes with that class.
- Notes: create note nodes with label text; add dashed edge `-.->` to target; class "note" for future styling.

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","title":"task(layout): implement deterministic fit-to-container and web-font metric lifecycle","description":"Background\n- xterm.js users expect reliable fit behavior plus font-loading integration without cursor drift or geometry jumps.\n- FrankenTermJS currently lacks an explicit bead for this parity surface.\n\nScope\n- Implement deterministic fit-to-container API with host callbacks/resize observation contract.\n- Define font-load invalidation pipeline: metric recompute, cache invalidation, patch regeneration boundaries, and throttling.\n- Handle DPR/zoom/container resizes without off-by-one geometry drift.\n\nAcceptance\n- Equivalent resize/font-load event streams yield identical viewport + cursor geometry outcomes.\n- No unbounded resize queue growth under storm conditions.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Fit API behavior is deterministic for fixed input streams (resize, font-load, DPR changes).\n- Unit tests cover metric recompute ordering, cache invalidation, and queue-throttling edge cases.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate resize storms, zoom transitions, and delayed web-font loads with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- Geometry drift, cursor misalignment, and stale-glyph artifacts are regression-gated in CI.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep metric and fit logic in deterministic Rust/WASM state; JS host should remain thin.\n- Explicitly document rounding/tie-break policies for fractional geometry to avoid browser divergence.\n- Add failure diagnostics for font-load race windows and resize burst coalescing decisions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:27.908441965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T00:53:14.651803885Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T00:53:14.651785019Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fit","frankenterm","layout","renderer","wasm","web-fonts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:27.908441965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:44.295223338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:43.930817274Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","title":"task(links): implement OSC-8 + URL-provider hyperlink detection and annotation pipeline","description":"Background\n- xterm.js web-links usage depends on both OSC-8 hyperlinks and plain-text URL detection/provider behavior.\n- FrankenTermJS needs deterministic hyperlink discovery and annotation across viewport + scrollback.\n\nScope\n- Preserve OSC-8 hyperlink metadata through parser/state/grid/scrollback paths.\n- Implement deterministic URL detection/provider API with explicit ordering and conflict policy versus OSC-8.\n- Expose hyperlink annotation data for hover, keyboard navigation, and secure activation layers.\n\nAcceptance\n- Hyperlink ranges remain stable under scroll, resize, and reflow operations.\n- Detection conflicts resolve deterministically with documented precedence.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- OSC-8 and URL-provider detection semantics are deterministic and documented (precedence, overlap, invalidation).\n- Unit tests cover hyperlink range stability under unicode graphemes, wraps, scrollback trims, and viewport reflow.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate hover/focus annotation correctness with structured JSONL logs.\n- Differential checks versus xterm.js hyperlink fixtures are wired into CI for covered scenarios.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep link annotations in core deterministic state so selection/copy/search can consume the same canonical ranges.\n- Explicitly log overlap-resolution decisions (OSC-8 vs provider URL) for postmortem clarity.\n- Do not allow hyperlink metadata paths to introduce unbounded per-cell payload growth.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyGate","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:51:52.228283813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T15:15:14.197071725Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T15:15:14.197037651Z","close_reason":"Implemented  {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","title":"task(image): implement bounded inline-image protocol decoding and cell-anchored rendering","description":"Background\n- xterm.js image addon users rely on inline image protocols for modern terminal workflows.\n- FrankenTermJS needs first-party, policy-driven support with hard resource bounds.\n\nScope\n- Parse and validate selected inline image protocol envelopes (capability-gated).\n- Implement deterministic image decode/upload pipeline with strict memory/frame budgets and eviction policy.\n- Render cell-anchored images with correct scroll/resize/viewport behavior and fallback semantics when capabilities are unavailable.\n\nAcceptance\n- Image rendering remains deterministic for fixed inputs/capability profiles.\n- Decode/render paths enforce bounded memory and queue usage under adversarial input.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Protocol parsing/validation for supported inline-image formats is deterministic, capability-gated, and fuzz/regression tested.\n- Unit tests cover decode limits, eviction policy, anchor semantics, and resize/scroll interactions.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate image-heavy workloads and failure modes (oversized payloads, malformed metadata) with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- CI gates fail on budget regressions (memory/frame time) or semantic drift in image positioning behavior.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep all image protocol support explicitly capability-gated and policy-controlled by defaults.\n- Enforce hard decode/upload caps before allocation; fail deterministically with reason-coded diagnostics.\n- Ensure image rendering never bypasses one-writer/frame-budget invariants.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":300,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:12.087634054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T05:36:52.374427753Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T05:36:52.374343726Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","image","protocol","renderer","security","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:25.010551183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:12.087634054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:24.645156728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:25.371523095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","title":"task(progress): implement deterministic progress signaling API and OSC-mapped semantics","description":"Background\n- xterm.js progress addon usage depends on exposing terminal-originated progress signals to host UI.\n- FrankenTermJS should provide a first-party deterministic progress event surface without addon coupling.\n\nScope\n- Define supported progress signal sources (including mapped escape-sequence forms) and normalization rules.\n- Expose typed progress events with ordering guarantees and bounded delivery semantics.\n- Integrate with host API docs and capability flags.\n\nAcceptance\n- Equivalent input/replay streams produce identical progress event sequences.\n- Unsupported/prohibited signals fail deterministically with traceable diagnostics.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Progress signal taxonomy and normalization rules are explicit, deterministic, and versioned.\n- Unit tests cover sequence ordering, dedupe/coalescing policy, invalid signal handling, and capability gating.\n- Browser E2E scripts validate host-visible progress lifecycle behavior with structured JSONL diagnostics.\n- API contract tests ensure typed event payload stability and error semantics.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Keep progress events in the same deterministic ordering model as other host events.\n- Do not leak unbounded high-frequency progress updates; enforce bounded delivery/coalescing policy.\n- Include trace IDs linking parsed escape input to emitted host events.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyGate","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:31.771885449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T15:43:20.495571540Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","events","frankenterm","parser","progress","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:42.364874191Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:42.787264441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:43.211698509Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:31.771885449Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","title":"task(text-render): implement optional ligature/shaping path with deterministic fallback","description":"Background\n- Many xterm.js users depend on ligature rendering for code readability in modern fonts.\n- FrankenTermJS should support this as an explicit, optional capability without sacrificing deterministic core behavior.\n\nScope\n- Add optional ligature/shaping render path with stable feature flags and fallback to canonical grapheme rendering.\n- Define cache/invalidation behavior for shaped clusters and font changes.\n- Ensure selection/cursor/extraction semantics remain correct when ligature shaping is enabled.\n\nAcceptance\n- Enabling/disabling ligature mode produces predictable, documented rendering differences only.\n- Core editing/navigation semantics remain invariant across ligature settings.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Ligature/shaping mode is capability-gated, deterministic, and documented with explicit fallback semantics.\n- Unit tests cover shaping cache invalidation, cursor/selection boundary correctness, and copy/extract invariants under ligature mode.\n- Browser E2E scripts validate ligature-enabled workflows across representative fonts with structured JSONL logs.\n- Performance and memory impacts are benchmarked and gated to prevent regressions.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- Treat ligatures as a rendering concern; canonical text storage and terminal semantics must remain unchanged.\n- Document and test the exact mapping between shaped glyphs and logical cell boundaries for interaction correctness.\n- Ensure fallback path is always available and deterministic when shaping prerequisites are missing.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":210,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:50.541282675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:54:02.598328717Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenterm","ligatures","renderer","text","unicode","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:52:50.541282675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:01.684144315Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:00.938379165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:01.315825142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.14.6","title":"task(test): add addon-parity compatibility harness (unit + browser E2E + differential + structured logs)","description":"Background\n- Addon-parity work must be validated as rigorously as core terminal behavior to avoid regressions and migration surprises.\n\nScope\n- Add focused unit/property tests for fit, links, image, progress, ligature, and web-font lifecycle invariants.\n- Add deterministic browser E2E scripts for representative workflows and adversarial cases.\n- Add differential harness coverage versus xterm.js for scoped parity scenarios.\n- Emit structured JSONL diagnostics with trace IDs and replay metadata.\n\nAcceptance\n- CI fails on semantic drift, non-deterministic outcomes, or missing diagnostics across addon-parity tracks.\n- Test artifacts are sufficient for deterministic replay and root-cause analysis.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Comprehensive unit tests exist for all addon-parity sub-capabilities in this feature and include edge/failure paths.\n- Deterministic browser E2E scripts cover core workflows and adversarial scenarios for each sub-capability.\n- Differential runs against xterm.js are integrated for scoped parity scenarios with actionable structured diff output.\n- CI gating fails on semantic drift, non-determinism, budget regression, or missing structured diagnostics.\n\n## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate\n- Comprehensive unit tests are required for implementation and edge-case regressions.\n- Deterministic E2E scripts are required for impacted workflows and must run in CI.\n- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (timestamps, correlation IDs, failure context) sufficient for deterministic replay.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- This bead is the enforcement gate for the entire addon-parity feature; do not mark done on partial coverage.\n- Ensure every failure artifact includes enough context to reproduce locally without hidden state.\n- Keep script interfaces stable so rollout/release beads can depend on canonical commands.\n- Expected deterministic E2E script set (minimum):\n  - tests/e2e/scripts/test_addon_fit_fonts.sh\n  - tests/e2e/scripts/test_addon_weblinks.sh\n  - tests/e2e/scripts/test_addon_image_protocols.sh\n  - tests/e2e/scripts/test_addon_progress_events.sh\n  - tests/e2e/scripts/test_addon_ligatures.sh\n  - tests/e2e/scripts/test_addon_parity_all.sh\n- Each script must emit JSONL traces under a canonical artifact directory and include replay seed + capability profile metadata.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:09.361336391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:45.609521151Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["differential","e2e","frankenterm","logging","parity","test","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:24.208823629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:09.361336391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:22.343690838Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:22.711296034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:23.083132605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:23.460536765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.14.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.14.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T07:53:23.828144676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.15","title":"epic(frankenterm-js): deterministic rescale + TeX-class real-time typography at 60+ fps","description":"North Star\n- Eliminate remaining web rescale correctness bugs while delivering a world-class text layout engine (kerning, leading, line-break, and justification quality) that feels immediate at 60+ fps.\n\nFirst Principles\n- Correctness before cleverness: layout must be deterministic for fixed inputs and capability profile.\n- Text quality is objective-driven: spacing, breaks, and vertical rhythm are optimized with explicit cost functions, not ad-hoc heuristics.\n- Performance is budget-governed: every frame and memory path has measurable constraints and fallback tiers.\n- Graceful degradation is automatic: overload never causes catastrophic UX failure or semantic corruption.\n\nProgram Scope\n- Web rescale hardening (ResizeObserver/DPR/zoom/font-load race windows).\n- TeX-class paragraph layout core (incremental Knuth-Plass + hyphenation + justification).\n- Shaping/kerning pipeline integration (cluster-safe cursor/selection/copy semantics).\n- Adaptive quality controller under compute pressure (controller + telemetry + evidence).\n- Exhaustive validation (unit/property/fuzz/browser E2E + structured JSONL evidence).\n\nOutcomes\n- No geometry drift, stale glyph artifacts, or resize-induced interaction corruption.\n- High-quality justified text with stable, deterministic line breaks and spacing.\n- 60+ fps sustained on representative workloads with explicit degradation policy when budgets are violated.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria\n- Rescale correctness failures are reproducible via deterministic traces and closed by regression tests.\n- TeX-class layout quality features (kerning/leading/justification/line-break quality) ship behind explicit capability policy with deterministic fallback.\n- Performance budgets and degradation tiers are implemented, measured, and enforced by CI.\n- Comprehensive unit/property/fuzz tests and browser E2E scripts exist with high-signal JSONL logs and deterministic replay metadata.\n- Release evidence bundles prove correctness + quality + performance under normal and overload conditions.","notes":"Execution Notes\n- This epic intentionally fuses correctness, typography quality, and performance engineering into one system-level program.\n- Existing relevant bug/parity threads to cross-reference during implementation: bd-2enu3 and bd-2vr05.14.1.\n- Use evidence-ledger mindset: each algorithmic decision should expose rationale and measurable impact.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":7200,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:38.215451002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:38.667176306Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["degradation","frankenterm","layout","performance","typography","wasm","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:40:38.215451002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+### Progress Signal Mapping (`terminal.progress`)
+
+`terminal.progress` is emitted from `feed()` when OSC `9;4` control sequences
+complete (BEL/ST terminator observed), using deterministic normalization:
+
+- `OSC 9;4;0;...` → `state="remove"`, `value=0`
+- `OSC 9;4;1;N` → `state="normal"`, `value=clamp(N,0..100)` (missing `N` defaults to `0`)
+- `OSC 9;4;2;N` → `state="error"`, `value` uses last known value when `N` missing/`0`
+- `OSC 9;4;3;...` → `state="indeterminate"`, `value` uses last known value
+- `OSC 9;4;4;N` → `state="warning"`, `value` uses last known value when `N` missing/`0`
+
+Malformed OSC `9;4` payloads emit deterministic rejection envelopes
+(`accepted=false`, `reason=...`) for traceable host diagnostics.
+
 ### Ordering Contract

@@ -0,0 +1,161 @@
+# Pane Parity Contract and Execution Program
+
+Status: Draft (execution in progress)  
+Owners: `ftui-demo-showcase`, `ftui-showcase-wasm`, `ftui-web`, `ftui-layout`  
+Last Updated: 2026-02-16
+
+## 1. Why This Exists
+
+FrankenTUI's architectural intent is terminal-first: the terminal path is canonical, and host variants (WASM/web) are adapters that preserve canonical behavior while optionally adding host niceties.
+
+A divergence currently exists:
+
+- Advanced pane interaction semantics (organic resize, reflow move, dock preview, group semantics, inertia, timeline operations) are implemented in the WASM runner path.
+- Terminal/demo rendering remains screen-local and does not consume the same pane engine semantics as the WASM path.
+
+This creates behavioral drift risk and violates the project objective of deterministic, equivalent behavior across hosts.
+
+## 2. Problem Statement
+
+Current behavior indicates two semantic paths:
+
+- WASM-specific pane interaction pipeline in `crates/ftui-showcase-wasm/src/runner_core.rs`.
+- Terminal screen rendering and interaction dispatch in `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` + per-screen `view()`/`update()` implementations.
+
+The result is not a guaranteed 1:1 behavior model between terminal and web for pane interaction semantics.
+
+## 3. Parity Contract (Normative)
+
+These rules are mandatory:
+
+1. Terminal is canonical for semantics.
+2. Web/WASM is an adapter, not an alternate semantics engine.
+3. Pane interaction semantics must have exactly one source of truth in shared code.
+4. Host-specific logic is limited to:
+   - event capture/lifecycle quirks (for example pointer capture in browsers),
+   - host-only visual overlays (for example glow/halo),
+   - transport/serialization.
+5. Undo/redo/replay event logs must be deterministic and host-portable.
+6. Workspace snapshot import/export must be schema-identical and semantically equivalent across hosts.
+7. Any pane-related feature change must include parity tests across terminal and web adapters.
+
+## 4. Scope
+
+In scope:
+
+- Pane movement, resizing (including edge/corner semantics), group operations.
+- Magnetic docking, ghost preview, pressure-sensitive snap.
+- Inertial settle behavior.
+- Adaptive layout intelligence modes (`Focus`, `Compare`, `Monitor`, `Compact`).
+- Persistent timeline, undo/redo/replay, snapshot import/export.
+- Dashboard and cross-screen integration policy.
+
+Out of scope:
+
+- Cosmetic-only host polish that does not alter semantic state transitions.
+
+## 5. Architectural Boundaries
+
+### 5.1 Shared Core (must be host-agnostic)
+
+- Pane interaction state machine and state model.
+- Layout operation planning and application orchestration over `PaneTree`.
+- Policy functions (snap, magnetic field, threshold hysteresis, spring blend).
+- Timeline mutation and rollback semantics.
+- Snapshot and replay semantics.
+
+### 5.2 Host Adapter Layer
+
+- Input normalization from host events into canonical pane events.
+- Pointer capture lifecycle (browser).
+- Terminal-specific event quirks.
+
+### 5.3 Presentation Layer
+
+- Rendering overlays from shared state (ghosts, halo, hints).
+- Styling and animation details that do not alter semantics.
+
+## 6. Determinism Requirements
+
+For equivalent event traces:
+
+- final topology hash must match,
+- timeline length/cursor must match,
+- operation sequence must match,
+- replay result must match.
+
+Any mismatch is a parity bug.
+
+## 7. Dashboard and Screen Integration Policy
+
+The dashboard is the priority integration target and must use shared pane semantics.
+
+For all showcase screens, each must be explicitly classified:
+
+- integrated with shared pane shell, or
+- intentionally excluded with written technical rationale and future plan.
+
+No implicit/unknown status is allowed.
+
+## 8. Migration Program (Execution Order)
+
+## Phase A: Contract + Shared Primitive Extraction
+
+- Land this contract and make it authoritative.
+- Extract shared pane semantic primitives from WASM-owned files into shared demo/core modules.
+- Keep behavior unchanged while reducing duplication.
+
+## Phase B: Shared Engine Consolidation
+
+- Move pane interaction controller/state from WASM-owned runner into shared module.
+- Make both terminal and WASM use the same shared controller.
+
+## Phase C: Terminal Canonical Wiring
+
+- Route terminal mouse/pointer/wheel/modifier events through the shared pane controller.
+- Ensure timeline/undo/redo/replay and mode transitions are driven by shared controller.
+
+## Phase D: WASM Adapter Reduction
+
+- Retain only adapter concerns in WASM path.
+- Remove duplicate semantic logic from WASM runner.
+
+## Phase E: Screen Integration Completion
+
+- Integrate dashboard and prioritized high-value screens.
+- Produce full screen integration status matrix.
+
+## Phase F: Proof + Enforcement
+
+- Add parity replay/golden suites.
+- Add CI guard requiring parity evidence for pane-related changes.
+
+## 9. Acceptance Criteria for “Parity Achieved”
+
+All of the following must be true:
+
+1. One shared pane semantics implementation exists.
+2. Terminal and web both consume it.
+3. Dashboard uses shared pane semantics in runtime behavior.
+4. Screen coverage status is complete and explicit (integrated or justified exclusion).
+5. Deterministic parity test suite passes.
+6. Snapshot portability and replay equivalence pass.
+7. Documentation and contributor guidance enforce no future divergence.
+
+## 10. Risks and Mitigations
+
+Risk: regressions during extraction.  
+Mitigation: staged migration with invariant checks and replay parity tests per phase.
+
+Risk: hidden host event differences.  
+Mitigation: strict canonical event normalization and trace comparison tooling.
+
+Risk: partial rollout ambiguity across screens.  
+Mitigation: explicit screen integration matrix with ownership and deadlines.
+
+## 11. Implementation Notes for Future Contributors
+
+- Prefer moving code, not re-implementing semantics in parallel.
+- If a host needs a visual enhancement, derive from shared state rather than introducing alternate logic.
+- If a behavior appears host-dependent, add a canonical event/adapter mapping rather than branching semantics.
+
